text: Figure 12.4 Self-attention in matrix form. Self-attention can be implemented efficiently if we store the N input vectors $x_{n}$ in the columns of the D×N matrix X. The input X is operated on separately by the query matrix Q, key matrix K, and value matrix V. The dot products are then computed using matrix multiplication, and a softmax operation is applied independently to each column of the resulting matrix to calculate the attentions. Finally, the values are post-multiplied by the attentions to create an output of the same size as the input.
